Grantee Database

Explore our comprehensive database of current and past grantees. The majority of grant partners receive unrestricted, multiyear grants.

Our Initiatives

Connecting People Outside

Connecting historically excluded communities to the outdoors - cultivating joy, healing, belonging, connection and well-being.

$18M+

Donated to date

Creating Space Outside

Ensuring people have equitable access to quality, close to home culturally-relevant outdoor spaces.

$10M+

Donated to date

Centering Health Outside

Strengthening and amplifying research that demonstrates nature is critical for individual and community health and well-being.

$7M+

Donated to date

Grantee Database

This database undergoes regular updates. The list below only includes organizational headquarter locations, not chapter locations. Grantees that do not have a color tag offer broad support across initiatives. And finally, we also encourage you to visit our Impact page to view our 990s for a list of grantees by year.

No grantees match your selected filters. Try adjusting your criteria.

    $20,000

    About

    Total grants: $20,000

    AL
    -

    Location: Huntsville, AL

    Current Grant:

    About

    Black Kids Adventure's mission is to expose, educate and empower black and brown families through nature-based and outdoor recreational activities. Building on our three pillars: exposure, education, and empowerment, BKA helps families connect to nature and each other. We believe greater outdoor diversity comes through early exposure. When we engage families, we can capture our youth and assist in cultivating a profound love for nature and all things outdoors.

    -

    About

    The mission of CicLAvia is to catalyze vibrant public spaces, active transportation, and good health through car-free open street events. CicLAvia engages with people throughout Southern California to transform relationships with our communities and with each other. CicLAvia temporarily closes streets to car traffic and opens them to use as a public park. Free for all to attend, CicLAvia connects communities to each other, creating safe places to bike, walk, skate, and dance through Los Angeles.

    Fort Collins
    CO
    -
    $30,000

    Location: Fort Collins, CO

    Current Grant:

    About

    The mission of Diversify Whitewater (DW) is to promote Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion (DEI) in paddle sports by removing the barriers that may exist for Black, Indigenous, People of Color (BIPOC) and Allies in kayaking, canoeing, rafting, and stand-up paddleboarding. Our ultimate goal is to build a cohort of BIPOC and ally boaters who can pass on their skills to the next generation and increase representation in the paddle sports community.

    About

    Eagle Mount is committed to providing quality adaptive recreation and sport opportunities for people with disabilities and young people impacted by cancer, and to provide support for families of participants, so that “they shall mount up with wings as eagles.” Programs include aquatics, horsemanship, Big Sky Kids oncology camps, family engagement and arts, and adaptive recreation. Eagle Mount recognizes disabilities and focuses on abilities because “Everybody has challenges, we have adventures."

Our mission is to restore and improve the Emerald Necklace for all.     Friends of the Riverfront builds safe, clean, and accessible riverfront trails as part of the Three Rivers Heritage & Water Trail systems to create and maintain an outstanding experience for trail users while providing economic, environmental, and health benefits to the Pittsburgh region. We maintain more than 33 miles of public riverfront trail and in Allegheny County, and continue to expand our trail system in the Pittsburgh region while providing support and programming for trail users.

    Total grants: $20,000

    Initiative

    Creating Space Outside

    OR
    -
    $20,000

    Location: Portland, OR

    Current Grant:

    About

    Friends of Trees inspires people to improve the world around them through a simple solution: Planting trees. Together. Since 1989 Friends of Trees has planted 945,000+ trees and native shrubs in neighborhoods & natural areas in six counties across two states. We’ve done this through engaging tens of thousands of community volunteers, and while implementing & growing programming that aims to do this work inclusively and equitably.

    -
    $20,000

    Location: San Diego, CA

    About

    Groundwork works with a broad coalition of partners and stakeholders to address environmental health, climate change resiliency, and food justice. Activities include the development of creek trails and parks; the restoration of creeks and canyons; neighborhood-scale investments in climate mitigation; and the education of youth and families in environmental stewardship and healthy living. Groundwork San Diego operates a 4-acre school site open space parcel soon to house a vermiponics farm.

    NY
    -
    $100,000

    About

    Groundwork USA is a national network of environmental justice organizations–Groundwork Trusts–working to create a world in which everyone can live in a healthy, green, and resilient community. To realize this vision, Trusts work to undo the legacy of environmental injustice by working hand-in-hand with residents to transform neglected land and waterways into community assets, like parks, trails, and community gardens, that meet their environmental, social, economic, and health priorities.

    KS Wild’s mission is to protect and restore wild nature in the Klamath-Siskiyou region of southwest Oregon and northwest California. We promote science-based land and water conservation through policy and community action. We envision a Klamath-Siskiyou region where local communities enjoy healthy wildlands, where clean rivers are teeming with native salmon, and where connected plant and wildlife populations are prepared for climate change.

    Total grants: $10,000

    Initiative

    Creating Space Outside

    Total grants: $125,000

    -

    About

Every acre saved cools and cleans the air we breathe, soaks up floodwaters, provides wildlife with habitat, filters the water we drink, and brings humans improved health and joy. For 70 years, Natural Lands has grown its impact. We are guided by the heart of our mission, land for life, nature for all—a promise we pursue by focusing on three core areas: saving open space, caring for nature, and connecting people to the outdoors.

Our Ocean.

    About

    Whiteswan Environmental (WE) supports community healing through the natural, cultural and historical restoration to the Salish Sea for 7th generations sustainability as a measure of ecological health protection for all. Vision: WE are the vision keepers for a Coast Salish Tribal Heritage Field Institute: an Indigenous-led, 13-Moon, Mountain to Sea, Reef to Reef, K-PhD program with 7 longhouses in the San Juan Islands and 7 longhouses in the Gulf Islands.

    About

(W.A.K.E., Inc.) provides mentoring, scholarships, and outdoor experience programs for youth in grades K-12 from low-income and marginalized communities, particularly youth in grades 5 - 8. W.A.K.E., Inc. was created to foster a commitment to youth that will promote pro-social friendships, strong interpersonal skills, resiliency, and self-awareness while developing outdoor sportsman skills.

Your donation supports nonprofits across the country working to create a world where everyone can experience the health and well-being benefits of nature.
